name: init-artifacts
description: mulch init creates .mulch/, config, .gitattributes (merge=union for JSONL), and .mulch/README.md — modeled after Beads' bd init pattern

name: git-hook-markers
description: Git hooks use # mulch:start / # mulch:end markers for safe append/remove in .git/hooks/pre-commit, same marker pattern as HTML comments in AGENTS.md sections

name: record-dedup
description: mulch record checks for duplicates before appending. Named types (pattern/decision) upsert by name/title. Unnamed types (convention/failure) skip on exact content/description match. --force overrides.

name: add-command
description: How to add a new CLI command: create src/commands/<name>.ts, export register<Name>Command, register in src/cli.ts

name: type-extensible-records
description: Adding record types follows a checklist: (1) record.ts types, (2) record-schema.ts oneOf, (3) record.ts command switch, (4) edit.ts switch, (5) expertise.ts findDuplicate, (6) format.ts formatters (md/xml/plain), (7) search.ts choices. Named types (pattern/decision/reference/guide) upsert by name; unnamed (convention/failure) skip on exact match.

name: tags-on-records
description: Optional tags: string[] on all record types via BaseRecord. --tags flag on record (comma-separated), --tag filter on search (exact match, case-insensitive). searchRecords also searches array values via Array.isArray check.

name: context-aware-prime
description: mulch prime --context filters records by git changed files. Records without files field (conventions, failures, decisions, guides) always pass. Pattern/reference records filtered by file-path intersection using suffix matching. filterByContext and fileMatchesAny live in src/utils/git.ts as shared utilities.

name: record-linking
description: relates_to and supersedes optional string[] fields on BaseRecord. Schema validates mx-ID pattern. formatLinks() helper renders in all prime formats (md, compact, xml, plain). Links shown even without --full flag.

name: compact-command
description: mulch compact --analyze finds candidates (3+ records of same type, or 2+ with stale records). --apply takes --records (IDs/indices), type-specific fields, removes source records, appends consolidated record with foundational classification and supersedes links.

name: compact-default-prime
description: mulch prime defaults to compact output. Use -v/--verbose for full output with section headers, rules, and recording instructions. --full shows expanded record details (classification, evidence, grouped sections). --format xml|plain bypass compact and use their own formatters.

name: messaging-sync
description: All user-facing messaging (onboard snippets, setup provider recipes, CLAUDE.md, README, CLI help text) uses unified template: 'At the start of every session', all 6 record types, context injection line, and mulch --help reference. Terminology standardized on 'records' not 'entries'.

content: Use 'records' not 'entries' in all user-facing messages (command descriptions, output text, format headers). Config field names like max_entries and XML attributes are exempt.
cli.ts .version() must match package.json version. Bump both together.

name: session-end-prompting
description: All agent-facing snippets (onboard, setup recipes, CLAUDE.md) use 'Before completing your task, review your work for insights' framing to prompt agents to record learnings as a gate before task completion — not a passive suggestion. The onboard snippet has a 'Before You Finish' checklist; provider recipes use a single-line instruction.

title: ID-based record addressing
rationale: Replaced 1-based index addressing with mx-ID prefix matching (like beads). edit/delete/compact all use resolveRecordId() from expertise.ts. IDs shown in all output formats (query, search, prime compact/full/xml/plain). Fixes mismatch where query type-groups display order differed from insertion-order indices.

name: onboard-marker-update
description: mulch onboard uses <!-- mulch:start --> / <!-- mulch:end --> markers for idempotent section replacement. resolveTargetFile checks CLAUDE.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md in order. Legacy (pre-marker) snippets detected by header and migrated. --check flag for read-only inspection.

name: shared-markers
description: src/utils/markers.ts exports MARKER_START, MARKER_END, hasMarkerSection(), replaceMarkerSection(), removeMarkerSection(), wrapInMarkers(). Used by both onboard.ts and setup.ts for HTML comment marker management in markdown files.

name: update-version-check
description: mulch update uses npm view mulch-cli version via execSync (10s timeout) to check registry. compareSemver in src/utils/version.ts does 3-segment numeric comparison. Doctor integrates as last check (warn status, not fail). --check flag for dry run.
CI workflows ran npm test before npm run build, but update.test.ts shells out to node dist/cli.js which requires built output.
resolution: Move npm run build before npm test in both CI and publish workflows

name: atomic-writes
description: writeExpertiseFile uses temp file + rename for atomic JSONL writes: write to ${filePath}.tmp.${process.pid}, then fs.rename to final path. Prevents partial reads during concurrent prime calls.

name: file-locking
description: withFileLock(filePath, fn) in src/utils/lock.ts provides advisory file-level locking using O_CREAT|O_EXCL .lock files. 50ms retry interval, 5s timeout, 30s stale detection. All read-modify-write command paths wrapped.

name: concurrency-docs
description: Concurrency safety documentation lives in three places that must stay in sync: (1) README.md 'Concurrency & Multi-Agent Safety' section with command safety table, (2) CLAUDE.md 'Concurrency Safety' subsection under Architecture, (3) onboard snippet in src/commands/onboard.ts SNIPPET_DEFAULT.

content: When documenting command safety for multi-agent use, categorize commands into three tiers: fully safe (read-only), safe (locked writes), and serialize (setup ops). This matches the actual locking architecture.

Maintain CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog format) in repo root. Add entries under [Unreleased] as changes are made. When bumping version, move [Unreleased] items into a new version section with date, and add a compare link to the footer.

Keep README.md in sync with project state. When features are added, changed, or removed, update the relevant README sections (command reference, usage examples, feature descriptions) in the same commit or PR.

name: prime-budget
description: mulch prime uses --budget <tokens> (default 4000, chars/4 estimate) to cap output. Records prioritized by type (convention>decision>pattern>guide>failure>reference), then classification (foundational>tactical>observational), then recency. Budget logic lives in src/utils/budget.ts. --no-limit disables. MCP/JSON output is never budgeted.

name: prime-session-reminder
description: mulch prime appends a Session Close Protocol section (with 🚨 markers) to all non-MCP/JSON output. getSessionEndReminder(format) in src/utils/format.ts returns format-appropriate text (markdown, xml, plain). Reminder is always shown regardless of budget.

Merged external PR then added tests in a separate commit without co-authoring the contributor. Can't push to fork branches, so either ask the contributor to add tests before merging, or co-author the follow-up commit.
resolution: When adding follow-up commits for external PRs, always Co-Authored-By the original contributor. Better yet, request changes on the PR so they can add tests themselves.

name: diff-command
description: mulch diff shows expertise changes between git refs. Uses execFileSync to run git diff on .mulch/expertise/, parses unified diff output to extract added/removed JSONL records per domain. Pure functions parseExpertiseDiff and formatDiffOutput separate parsing logic from command registration for testability.

Used greedy regex /.mulch\/expertise\/(.+)\.jsonl/ to extract domain from git diff headers like 'diff --git a/.mulch/expertise/cli.jsonl b/.mulch/expertise/cli.jsonl', captured 'cli.jsonl b/.mulch/expertise/cli' instead of 'cli'.
resolution: Changed to non-greedy pattern /\.mulch\/expertise\/([^/]+)\.jsonl/ using [^/]+ to match only characters that aren't forward slashes, correctly extracting just the domain name.

name: status-json-health
description: mulch status --json includes domain health metrics: governance_utilization (percentage of max_entries), stale_count (using isRecordStale with shelf_life config), type_distribution (counts per RecordType), classification_distribution (counts per Classification), oldest_timestamp, newest_timestamp. Calculated via calculateDomainHealth in expertise.ts

name: confirmation-prompts
description: Use createInterface from node:readline for interactive confirmation prompts. Implement confirmAction helper that returns Promise<boolean>, check y/yes responses. Always provide --yes flag to skip confirmation in non-interactive/CI contexts.

name: dry-run-flag
description: For commands that perform bulk/destructive operations, add --dry-run flag to preview what would happen without writing. Show detailed summary of planned changes. In JSON mode, output 'action: dry-run' with structured data about what would be modified.

name: batch-operation-guardrails
description: For auto/batch operations, add guardrails: (1) minimum threshold (--min-group), (2) maximum limit per run (--max-records), (3) summary before execution, (4) confirmation prompt unless --yes. Prevents over-aggressive automation from destroying data/granularity.

Node v25.3.0 intercepts piped stdin (echo '...' | node script.js) and evaluates it as JavaScript instead of passing it to the process. Breaks: (1) pipe-based --stdin usage, (2) interactive confirmation prompts via pipe.
resolution: Use file redirects (< file.json) instead of pipes, or invoke via bin link (npx mulch). Consider adding --yes flag to delete command for non-interactive use.

name: domain-scoped-operations
description: When a CLI command accepts an optional domain argument and iterates over domains, filter to the specified domain if provided. Apply domain validation before processing. Pattern: const domainsToCheck = domain ? [domain] : config.domains;

name: dry-run-preview-detail
description: Dry-run modes should show detailed previews, not just summaries. Users need to see what will change (affected items, groups, records) to build confidence before running destructive operations. Show the same level of detail as the actual operation would display.

name: hierarchical-grouped-output
description: When displaying grouped data (e.g., by domain), organize output hierarchically with clear visual structure. Use bold headers for groups, indentation for hierarchy, and summary lines showing totals. Makes output easier to scan and understand at a glance.

name: doctor-details-output
description: For non-pass checks (warn/fail), print check.details array with indentation after the status message to show per-domain information like governance thresholds and actual values

name: record-type-extensibility
description: Record type extensibility via discriminated union: Each of 6 record types extends BaseRecord with type-specific required fields, validated via JSON schema oneOf. Adding new type requires: (1) TypeScript interface in record.ts, (2) schema oneOf variant in record-schema.ts, (3) switch cases in generateRecordId/findDuplicate (expertise.ts), (4) formatter functions in format.ts, (5) CLI options in edit.ts, (6) type choices in search.ts. Domain health, validation, compact/prune/doctor commands are generic and need no changes.

name: semantic-duplicate-detection
description: Duplicate detection uses semantic key matching: findDuplicate() in expertise.ts uses type-specific key fields to identify duplicates before append. Each type has its matching key: pattern→name, decision→title, convention→content, failure→description, reference→name, guide→name. New types must define their unique key field in a switch case to prevent duplicate records.

name: type-specific-formatting
description: Record type formatting uses dedicated type-specific formatters in format.ts: formatConventions, formatPatterns, formatFailures, formatDecisions, formatReferences, formatGuides. All called from formatDomainExpertise after filtering records by type. getRecordSummary() and compactLine() also have switch statements for compact prime output. Formatters use shared helpers: idTag(), formatRecordMeta(), formatLinks() to reduce duplication.

name: file-and-classification-filters
description: filterByClassification and filterByFile in expertise.ts follow the same filterByType pattern — accept ExpertiseRecord[] and a string, return filtered array. filterByFile uses 'files' in r guard (PatternRecord and ReferenceRecord have files?: string[]) and does case-insensitive substring match. --classification and --file options added to both search and query commands.

name: outcome-metadata
description: Outcome type (status: 'success'|'failure', duration?, test_results?, agent?) added as optional field on BaseRecord. Schema uses $ref '#/definitions/outcome'. CLI options: --outcome-status, --outcome-duration, --outcome-test-results, --outcome-agent. Filtering via --outcome-status in search.

name: scoring-module
description: src/utils/scoring.ts exports Outcome, ScoredRecord, and scoring functions: getSuccessCount, getFailureCount, getTotalApplications, getSuccessRate, computeConfirmationScore, applyConfirmationBoost, sortByConfirmationScore. Partial outcomes count as 0.5 in rate and score. applyConfirmationBoost multiplies baseScore by (1 + factor * confirmationScore). sortByConfirmationScore returns a new array (does not mutate input).

content: budget-scoring integration: widened DomainRecords.records from ExpertiseRecord[] to ScoredRecord[]; recordSortKey returns 4-tuple [typeIdx, classIdx, -confirmationScore, -time]. No breaking change since outcomes is optional.

name: search-sort-by-score
description: search command --sort-by-score: sortByConfirmationScore from scoring.ts integrated by casting ExpertiseRecord[] as ScoredRecord[] (valid since outcomes is optional) and applying after query/filter pipeline. Both JSON and non-JSON code paths apply the sort.

name: programmatic-api
description: src/api.ts exports recordExpertise, searchExpertise, queryDomain, editRecord as high-level programmatic wrappers over the expertise utilities. All four accept an optional cwd for path resolution, use withFileLock for writes, and throw descriptive errors on invalid domain/id.

name: branding-palette
description: Each tool defines src/utils/palette.ts with brand (tool-specific color), accent (amber for IDs), muted (stone gray), icons (✓/!/✗/−/>/x set D), setQuiet/isQuiet, printSuccess/printError/printWarning. Mulch brand: chalk.rgb(139,90,43). Shared: accent=chalk.rgb(255,183,77), muted=chalk.rgb(120,120,110). Import palette in commands instead of raw chalk.green/red.

content: cli.ts exports VERSION constant and handles --version --json early (before Commander), applies --quiet/-q early via setQuiet(). .version(VERSION, '-v, --version', 'Print version') uses short form -v. addHelpCommand(false) suppresses the default help subcommand.

name: help-style-A
description: Style A help screen via program.configureHelp({ formatHelp(cmd, helper) }): (1) header line with brand.bold(name) + muted version + tagline, (2) Usage line, (3) Commands: chalk.green(name) + chalk.dim(args) + plain-text padding to col 20, (4) Options: chalk.dim(flags) + padding to col 20, (5) footer 'Run dim(alias) <command> --help'. Padding uses rawLen (plain text), not ANSI length.

content: cli.ts exports VERSION constant and handles --version --json early (before Commander), applies --quiet/-q early via setQuiet(). Use .version(VERSION, '-v, --version', 'Print version') for short -v flag. addHelpCommand(false) suppresses default help subcommand.

name: upgrade-command
description: mulch upgrade checks npm registry via getLatestVersion(), compares with VERSION, optionally installs via Bun.spawnSync bun install -g @os-eco/mulch-cli@latest. Uses palette helpers for human output, outputJson for --json. --check flag reports without installing.

name: doctor-legacy-outcome-check
description: checkLegacyOutcome() in doctor.ts scans raw JSONL for records with singular 'outcome' field (legacy pre-outcomes[] format). Fix migrates outcome:{...} → outcomes:[{...}] via applyFixes case 'legacy-outcome', using same field extraction as readExpertiseFile normalization. Check inserted before schema-validation so --fix migrates before schema check removes the record. biome requires dot notation (raw.outcome) and undefined assignment instead of delete.

name: validate-legacy-outcome-warning
description: validate command detects records with legacy 'outcome' (singular) field and emits a warning (not an error) since readExpertiseFile already normalizes at read time. JSON output includes totalWarnings and warnings[] alongside totalErrors and errors[]. Text output prints yellow warning lines and includes warning count in summary. Legacy records skip Ajv schema validation (which would fail due to additionalProperties:false).

name: auto-create-domain
description: mulch record auto-creates a domain (addDomain in config.ts) when the domain doesn't exist in config, instead of failing. addDomain: validateDomainName, readConfig, push domain, writeConfig, createExpertiseFile. Called from both processStdinRecords and CLI-mode action.

name: validation-type-hints
description: RECORD_TYPE_REQUIREMENTS map in record.ts maps record type to required-fields string. Appended as '. Hint: <hint>' to AJV validation errors in processStdinRecords and CLI-mode schema validation fallback. No hint added when record has no type field.

name: domain-not-found-hints
description: When a domain arg is not found in text mode, print a second console.error line: 'Hint: Run `mulch add <name>` to create this domain, or check .mulch/mulch.config.yaml'. Applies to query (domain arg), search (--domain), prime (domain arg and --exclude-domain). JSON mode does not get the hint — text-only.

name: typo-suggestions
description: Typo suggestions via Levenshtein: editDistance() + suggestCommand() in cli.ts, with program.on("command:*") handler. showSuggestionAfterError(false) disables Commander built-in. Threshold: distance <= 2. Supports --json mode via outputJsonError.
File /Users/jayminwest/Projects/os-eco/mulch/.overstory/worktrees/src-cleanup-builder/src/commands/doctor.ts required 4 edits during session — high iteration suggests complexity

name: merge-conflict-recovery
description: overstory merge conflict recovery: When automated merge fails (tier: ai-resolve), the conflict is often caused by overlapping changes from same-session merges. Recovery: spawn a new lead on a fresh branch from current main, have it apply changes directly, then merge the clean branch. Closing the original issue and creating a fresh one avoids sling validation errors on closed tasks.

name: upgrade-testability-refactor
description: src/commands/upgrade.ts uses getCurrentVersion() from version.ts instead of importing VERSION constant from cli.ts. This allows tests to control the version without importing the full CLI module. (src/commands/upgrade.ts, src/utils/version.ts, test/commands/upgrade.test.ts)

name: delete-domain-command
description: mulch delete-domain <domain> removes domain from config (removeDomain in config.ts), deletes .mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl, requires --yes flag for non-interactive use. Uses withFileLock for concurrency safety. Registered in cli.ts.

name: v1-doc-expansion
description: When updating a V1_DONE.md scope/readiness doc: break checklists into functional sections (core commands, maintenance, onboarding, schema, safety, API, testing, CI); remove out-of-scope items that were actually implemented; add Known Edge Cases section for production risks; substantiate all claims with concrete evidence (line counts, test counts, version numbers).

name: worktree-aware-storage
description: getMulchDir() uses resolveWorktreeRoot() (git rev-parse --git-common-dir) to redirect to main repo .mulch/ when inside a git worktree. isInsideWorktree() exported for sync guard. Sync skips commit in worktree context.

name: isInsideWorktree-submodule-fix
description: isInsideWorktree submodule false positive: --git-common-dir returns /parent/.git/modules/<name> for git submodules, which does NOT end with '.git'. Fixed by early-returning false when common dir doesn't end with '.git' — actual worktrees always get a common dir ending in '.git'. Test uses git -c protocol.file.allow=always submodule add for local file:// paths (blocked by git security since 2.38.1).

name: submodule-detection-worktree
description: Submodule detection in isInsideWorktree: git rev-parse --git-common-dir returns paths ending with .git for normal repos and worktrees, but /parent/.git/modules/<name> for submodules. Early-return false when common dir does not end with .git to avoid false positives.

name: doctor-file-anchors
description: checkFileAnchors() in doctor.ts scans every record's files[] (PatternRecord/ReferenceRecord) and evidence.file, resolves paths relative to cwd, warns on any that don't exist. --fix mode strips broken paths (removes from files[], sets evidence.file to undefined) without deleting records. Follows same shape as checkLegacyOutcome().

name: compact-line-meta
description: compactMeta(r) replaces compactId(r) to show id + classification age + ★score in each compact line. formatClassificationAge shows 'foundational' verbatim, 'tactical/observational' with formatTimeAgo age. computeConfirmationScore imported from scoring.ts.

content: prime compact Quick Reference: always include type→required-fields table, --relates-to in evidence line, and frame --files as 'before editing a file, not just session start'. These live in formatPrimeOutputCompact in format.ts.

name: evidence-multi-tracker
description: Evidence schema supports seeds/gh/linear tracker fields alongside bead. CLI flags: --evidence-seeds, --evidence-gh, --evidence-linear. Schema definition in src/schemas/record-schema.ts definitions.evidence.

name: git-context-auto-populate
description: src/utils/git-context.ts exports getCurrentCommit(cwd?) and getContextFiles(cwd?). record command auto-populates evidence.commit from HEAD if --evidence-commit omitted; auto-populates files from staged/unstaged changes if --files omitted for pattern/reference records.

name: outcomes-upsert-merge
description: When upserting a named record (pattern/decision/reference/guide), existing outcomes are preserved and new outcomes appended. Both batch (processStdinRecords) and single-record paths apply this merge. Record with no new outcomes still preserves existing ones.

name: validation-retry-hint
description: buildRetryCommand() in record.ts reconstructs the mulch record command from current options and appends missing-field placeholders. Called in non-JSON mode when type-specific required fields are absent. Format: 'Retry: mulch record <domain> --type <t> ... --missing-flag "<placeholder>"'.

name: onboard-default-target-fix
description: onboard fallback-target fix: when no snippet found and neither CLAUDE.md nor AGENTS.md exists, create CLAUDE.md (not AGENTS.md). If AGENTS.md already exists without a snippet, still append there to respect Codex-style projects. Change is in resolveTargetFile() in src/commands/onboard.ts.

content: Canonical CLI alias for mulch is 'ml' — all user-facing teaching surfaces (onboard snippet, setup recipes, prime output quick reference, session-end reminder, .mulch/README.md template) must use 'ml' not 'mulch'. CLAUDE.md and README.md already use 'ml'. See messaging-sync pattern mx-108581.

Teaching-surface commands in source output (console.log/error hints, check messages) follow the same 'ml' alias rule as onboard snippets and docs — test assertions must stay in sync when command names change.

name: non-null-assertion-removal
description: Replace ! non-null assertions in tests: use ?. for optional chaining (Biome unsafe auto-fix), as T for type assertions when TS knows value exists, and ?? fallback for numeric comparisons. Fix noNonNullAssertedOptionalChain errors (?.x!) by removing the trailing !. Fix TS2345 from ?. returning T|undefined by adding ?? default or as cast.

name: noUncheckedIndexedAccess-narrowing
description: With noUncheckedIndexedAccess:true, array[i] is T|undefined even in bounds loops. Fix patterns: (1) lines[i]??'' for string arrays where empty string is safe; (2) for...of with entries() to get typed element and index; (3) destructure tuples [a,b,c,d] instead of indexing; (4) early-return guard const x=arr[i]; if(!x) return/continue.

name: noNonNullAssertion-cleanup
description: Remove noNonNullAssertion warnings from test files: run 'bunx biome check --write --unsafe' first for FIXABLE cases (array index access like arr[0]?.[0]), then manually fix non-FIXABLE patterns: (1) spread contexts { ...arr[0]! } -> extract to named var with if-guard; (2) biome-introduced noNonNullAssertedOptionalChain (x?.y!) -> explicit two-step guard; (3) optional chain producing T|undefined where T required -> append ?? fallback.

content: noNonNullAssertion is 'error' in biome.json (not 'warn'). The lint script uses --error-on-warnings as a belt-and-suspenders guard. All ! non-null assertions removed from src/ and test/ — use optional chaining ?., early returns, or runtime guards instead.

name: lint-hardening-integrator-pattern
description: For large mechanical cleanup tasks (e.g. 448 noNonNullAssertion warnings) split across non-overlapping file scopes, a coordinator can dispatch 3+ parallel cleanup builders partitioned by file count and then a single 'integrator' lead-lite builder to merge the 3 branches, flip enforcement config, and run quality gates. Non-overlap is essential — conflicts from overlapping file ownership waste integrator cycles. The integrator pattern lets the cleanup builders stay focused while one agent owns the 'land + enforce' step. Evidence: mulch-0775 merged src-cleanup 71ce245 + test-heavy 3c369f2 + test-light 6c79d2c with zero conflicts, then flipped biome.json + package.json in one commit 22cf3f3.

content: Lint policy: bun run lint must exit non-zero on ANY biome diagnostic. biome.json locks the intent (style.noNonNullAssertion: error, same as other custom rules), and package.json's lint script adds --error-on-warnings as belt-and-suspenders for any future 'warn' rules. Do not reintroduce the 'warn' severity for this rule without first cleaning up every ! non-null assertion in src/ and test/.

description: Lead agent attempted to dispatch a builder with a rich mail body that contained literal phrases like 'git merge <branch>' and 'git checkout -- <file>'. The overstory PreToolUse shell hook inspects Bash tool JSON and blocks any command whose extracted text matches a destructive regex (git add/commit/merge/checkout/etc.). Even mail bodies trigger the block because the regex is content-based, not intent-based.
resolution: Rephrase dispatch specs to avoid literal destructive command tokens. Use 'merges' instead of 'git merge', 'revert via git' instead of 'git checkout --', 'land commits' instead of 'git commit'. The builder can still execute those commands in its own session; only the lead-side mail body needs to be sanitized.

name: toMatchObject-for-union-field-access
description: When testing ExpertiseRecord union type fields (content, name, title) under noUncheckedIndexedAccess, prefer expect(records[0]).toMatchObject({ field: value }) over (records[0] as { field: T }).field — eliminates the cast and gives richer error messages on failure.

name: noUncheckedIndexedAccess-id-narrowing
description: For array element id access under noUncheckedIndexedAccess: replace 'records[0]?.id as string' with 'const id = records[0]?.id; if (!id) throw new Error(...)' — preserves runtime safety and removes the cast. Use combined guard 'if (!id0 || !id2) throw' when multiple ids needed.

name: manifest-mode
description: ml prime --manifest emits Quick Reference + per-domain index (with per-record-type counts and governance status) instead of full records, for monolith projects. Configurable via prime.default_mode in mulch.config.yaml. --manifest + scoping arg (positional/--domain/--exclude-domain/--context/--files) is a hard error; scoping wins over config.prime.default_mode='manifest'. Session-close protocol always appended.

content: Optional config sections (e.g. prime?: { default_mode }) on MulchConfig must NOT be added to DEFAULT_CONFIG so that mulch init keeps emitting a clean config. Document the knob in MULCH_README and the ml onboard snippet for discovery instead.

name: xml-attr-escape
description: xmlEscape() escapes &/</> but NOT quotes, which is unsafe for XML attribute values that contain user-supplied or non-domain-name strings. Added xmlAttrEscape() in src/utils/format.ts that wraps xmlEscape and additionally replaces " with " — use it for attribute values, plain xmlEscape() for element text.

name: init-config-template
description: ml init writes mulch.config.yaml from buildInitialConfigYaml() (templated string in src/utils/config.ts) instead of yaml.dump(DEFAULT_CONFIG), so the scaffolded file includes a header comment and a commented-out optional-knobs section (e.g. prime.default_mode). Subsequent writeConfig() calls (addDomain, removeDomain, doctor --fix, etc.) still go through yaml.dump and strip comments — by design, the comment scaffold lives only at init time. To keep required-field values in sync with DEFAULT_CONFIG, the body is generated via yaml.dump(DEFAULT_CONFIG) and concatenated with HEADER + body + OPTIONAL_KNOBS.

name: boost-confirmation-search
description: ml search applies applyConfirmationBoost to BM25 scores by default. searchRecords accepts an optional { boostFactor } SearchRecordsOptions; the search command computes boostFactor from --no-boost flag → 0, else config.search.boost_factor ?? DEFAULT_SEARCH_BOOST_FACTOR (0.1). Boost only fires when query is present (boost makes no sense without BM25 scores).

name: global-format-flag
description: Global --format <markdown|compact|xml|plain> flag on program in src/cli.ts is read from program.opts() in record-rendering commands (prime, query, search). prime owns format resolution via resolvePrimeFormat() which honors precedence: global --format > --full alias > --compact alias > 'compact' default. query/search keep per-command --format with extended choices including 'ids', and the per-command flag wins over the global. Commander v14 supports the global flag both before AND after the subcommand on the cmdline.

content: When extending PrimeFormat union type, formatPrimeManifest and getSessionEndReminder switch on format and let new variants fall through to the default markdown branch — they don't need explicit cases for 'compact' since the manifest/session-end output is independent of compact-vs-markdown styling.

name: ajv-validator-cache
description: AJV validator is compiled once on TypeRegistry construction and exposed as registry.validator. Commands (record, edit, validate, doctor, compact, sync) read from getRegistry().validator instead of compiling per-command — eliminates compile-per-record cost on large JSONLs and prepares for Phase 2 dynamic schemas.

name: phase2-custom-types
description: Custom record types declared in mulch.config.yaml under custom_types: get full registry treatment via buildCustomTypeDefinition (src/registry/custom.ts). cli.ts calls await initRegistryFromConfig() at startup to merge BUILTIN_DEFS + custom defs into the live TypeRegistry; getRegistry() falls back to built-ins-only when no .mulch/ exists. Reserved names: the six built-ins. Required-field name regex: ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$. Required/optional fields cannot collide with base record fields (id, type, classification, recorded_at, evidence, tags, relates_to, supersedes, outcomes).

name: compact-strategy-dispatch
description: compact.ts mergeRecords dispatches via def.compact: 'concat' joins required string fields (LONGEST_PICK_FIELDS={name,title} take the longest value, others join with \n\n); 'keep_latest' picks max recorded_at; 'merge_outcomes' subgroups candidates by idKey in findCandidates and combines outcomes from all records sharing idKey; 'manual' throws to require user-driven mulch compact --apply. All built-ins use 'concat' (preserving the original mergeRecords behavior).

content: RecordType is now widened to string. Use BuiltinRecordType (the literal union of the six built-ins) when narrowing back is needed (e.g., TYPE_PRIORITY arrays in budget.ts/format.ts). For record discriminated narrowing, prefer registry.get(r.type)?.kind === 'builtin' followed by the appropriate cast.

name: dynamic-cli-flag-registration
description: registerRecordCommand and registerEditCommand walk registry.enabled() and call cmd.option(--<field-name-kebab> <field>) for every custom-type required/optional field not already declared by the built-in flag set. Field names get _ -> - for the flag (snake_case to kebab-case) and Commander auto-camelCases for options.<key> access via fieldToOptionKey().

name: phase3-alias-resolution-readers
description: Phase 3 unknown-type policy + aliases: applyAliases must run before Ajv in validate.ts, sync.ts, and doctor.ts checkSchemaValidation — they read raw bytes (not via readExpertiseFile), so without the explicit applyAliases call a record with a legacy field name fails validation right after a rename, defeating the schema-evolution feature.

title: phase3-aliases-canonical-to-legacy-direction
rationale: Phase 3 aliases use {canonical_field: [legacy_names]} direction (not the {legacy: [canonical]} the issue example suggested). Composes for fields renamed multiple times; matches array-value shape; canonical wins on conflict; legacy is dropped on read.

name: runtime-flags-module
description: Process-wide CLI runtime flags (quiet, allow-unknown-types) live as module-level singletons with set/is/reset helpers, set in cli.ts via raw-args scan BEFORE Commander parses. Mirrors the setQuiet/isQuiet pattern in palette.ts. New global flags follow this template — see src/utils/runtime-flags.ts and the early-detection block in src/cli.ts.

content: TypeRegistry disabled set does NOT filter enabled(): disabled types remain in CLI --type choices, Ajv validation, and registry.get() lookups. The isDisabled(name) bit only gates the write-time deprecation warning in record.ts. Don't filter them out of enabled() or you'll break shared-domain peers (overstory/greenhouse) that still write the type.

All readExpertiseFile calls in doctor.ts pass { allowUnknownTypes: true }: doctor is a diagnostic tool and must never crash on the records it's diagnosing. checkUnknownTypes flags them as a separate failing check. Future doctor extensions that read JSONL must follow this — applies to checkSchemaValidation's --fix branch too (filter, don't drop, unknown-type records).

description: estimateRecordText in prime.ts switched over the 6 built-in record types only; custom-typed records fell through and returned undefined, crashing the budget step in applyBudget via estimateTokens(undefined).length with the opaque error 'undefined is not an object (evaluating text.length)'. Slipped past tests because custom-types e2e never exercised ml prime.
resolution: Per-record string helpers (estimators, summaries, compact-line builders) must dispatch via getRegistry().get(record.type)?.formatCompactLine rather than a hardcoded type switch. Custom types and unknown-tolerated types (via --allow-unknown-types) then get coverage for free. Add a regression test that pushes a custom-typed record through applyBudget, not just the formatter in isolation.

name: lifecycle-hooks
description: src/utils/hooks.ts runHooks(event, payload, opts) executes config.hooks[event] scripts via 'sh -c'. Each script gets {event, payload} JSON on stdin. pre-* events block on non-zero exit; post-* events warn. pre-record and pre-prime allow stdout-JSON mutation (chained across scripts in array order); pre-prune is block-only per spec. Skipped in dry-run modes.

name: hook-subprocess-kill
description: Bun.spawn timeout option may not propagate SIGTERM through 'sh -c <forked-binary>' (sleep survives killing parent shell on macOS). For test fixtures simulating slow hooks, use shell-builtin busy loop ('while :; do :; done') instead of 'sleep N' so a SIGKILL on the parent terminates everything. In production hooks.ts, killSignal:'SIGKILL' upgrades after grace and proc.signalCode signals timeout.

name: async-closure-narrowing
description: TypeScript's flow analysis won't narrow a 'let x: T | null = null' variable mutated inside an async closure (e.g., withFileLock callback) because the closure return is opaque. Fix: have the closure RETURN the value and capture as 'const x = await withFileLock<T|null>(...)' — TS narrows correctly via the await result. Avoid the let-and-mutate pattern around await callbacks.

name: domains-object-map
description: MulchConfig.domains is Record<string, DomainConfig>, not string[]. Iterate via Object.keys(config.domains); membership check via 'd in config.domains'; add via 'config.domains[d] = {}'; remove via 'delete config.domains[d]'. readConfig normalizes legacy YAML array form ('domains: [a, b]') to object map ('{a: {}, b: {}}') so old configs keep working without user migration.

name: allowed-types-gate
description: Per-domain allowed_types[] in DomainConfig gates which record types may be written into a domain. Empty/missing list ⇒ all registered types allowed (back-compat). getAllowedTypes(config, domain) returns null for unrestricted, otherwise the list. Enforced in two paths in src/commands/record.ts: (1) registerRecordCommand action right after getRegistry().get(recordType), reject with retry hint via buildRetryCommand swapping --type to first allowed; (2) processStdinRecords inside the per-record validation loop, push 'Record N: type X is not allowed in domain Y. Allowed types: ...' into errors[].

title: disabled_types wins over allowed_types on overlap
rationale: If a domain's allowed_types includes a type that is ALSO in the global disabled_types list, the write succeeds with the existing deprecation warning (registry behavior wins). Cross-project safety: peer agents in shared domains shouldn't hard-fail when an upstream config retires a type. The allowed_types check rejects only when the type is missing from the per-domain list — disabled membership is a separate, softer signal.

name: required-fields-gate
description: Per-domain required_fields[] in DomainConfig stacks on top of per-type RECORD_TYPE_REQUIREMENTS in record.ts: validation runs after Ajv schema check (CLI flow) and after allowed_types check (processStdinRecords). Missing fields are surfaced in a single error listing all of them; CLI flow emits a copy-paste retry hint with --<field> placeholders for every missing field via buildRetryCommand. findMissingDomainFields treats undefined/null/""/empty-array as missing. Top-level fields only — nested paths (evidence.commit) are out of scope for v1.

title: domain required_fields stack, never replace per-type
rationale: required_fields adds top-level field requirements on top of per-type schema-required fields rather than replacing them. Two independent checks fire: Ajv schema validation enforces per-type required (e.g. task requires description), and the domain check enforces config-required (e.g. backend requires oncall_owner). A record missing per-type still fails on schema even when domain fields are present, so peer agents in other domains aren't affected by one domain's policy.

name: domain-rules-helpers
description: src/utils/domain-rules.ts exports getAllowedTypes, getRequiredFields, findMissingDomainFields — single-source helpers shared by record (write-time gate), validate + sync (read-time re-validation), and doctor (diagnostic surfacing). Empty/missing arrays mean 'no rule' (back-compat). Top-level field names only; nested paths out of scope for v1.

name: domain-conformance-doctor
description: doctor.ts checkDomainConformance returns paired checks: domain-conformance (informational, per-domain conforming/violating counts) and domain-violations (failing, lists 'domain:line [id] (type)' with offending rule). Single shared collector reads JSONL once. No --fix in v1 — violations require human judgment to rewrite vs. relax.

name: sync-domain-revalidate
description: sync.ts validateExpertise() runs allowed_types / required_fields checks against on-disk records after registry init. Re-reads config from disk so worktree/CI lag (JSONL via merge=union) reconciles once config catches up. Sync intentionally ignores --allow-domain-mismatch (matches --allow-unknown-types convention: escape hatches stop at commit gate).

title: domain-violations not auto-fixable in v1
rationale: Violations of allowed_types/required_fields require human judgment: rewrite the record to fit the domain, or relax the config. Auto-removing or auto-rewriting risks data loss or config drift. doctor prints them for human triage; sync blocks commits until resolved.

name: test-id-hex-pattern
description: Mulch record IDs follow ^mx-[0-9a-f]{4,8}$ — only hex chars allowed. Test fixture IDs that include letters g-z (e.g. 'mx-tight', 'mx-flag', 'mx-no-own') fail Ajv schema validation with /id must match pattern error. Use hex-only fillers like mx-deadbe, mx-c0ffee, mx-faceb0 in tests.

name: dir-anchors-baseSchemaProps
description: dir_anchors[] is wired into BOTH src/schemas/record-schema.ts (the static reference schema) AND src/registry/builtins.ts baseSchemaProps + src/registry/custom.ts (the per-type ajvSchemas the runtime validator actually uses). Adding a new BaseRecord field requires touching both — the registry validator is built from each TypeDefinition.ajvSchema, not from recordSchema.

name: dir-anchor-auto-pop-heuristic
description: Auto-population threshold for dir_anchors is 3+ files sharing an immediate parent directory (configurable via inferDirAnchors threshold arg, default 3). Only counts immediate parents — files distributed across grandchildren of a common ancestor don't trigger an anchor at the ancestor. Explicit --dir-anchor wins over the heuristic.

content: Path normalization for dir_anchors: trailing slashes stripped on write ('src/foo/' → 'src/foo'), '.' and '' collapse to repo root (stored as nothing). fileLivesUnderDir does a boundary-respecting prefix check so 'src/util' does NOT match 'src/utils/foo.ts'.

name: extends-builtin-inheritance
description: Custom types may declare 'extends: <builtin>' to inherit required/optional/dedup_key/id_key/summary/compact/section_title/extracts_files/files_field from one of the six built-ins. mergeFieldList() unions parent+child arrays preserving parent order; child fields listed under required are stripped from optional (parent-optional → child-required promotion). Each effective property uses 'cfg.X ?? parent?.X ?? default'. The AJV schema's type const is overridden to the child's name; parent props + child props merge into one ajvSchema object. Custom-from-custom and extending a disabled built-in are hard errors.

content: Template strings in src/utils/config.ts (INIT_CONFIG_OPTIONAL_KNOBS, MULCH_README, etc.) must not contain raw backticks — they terminate the template literal and crash bun at module load (tsc accepts it, so the bug only surfaces when the CLI is spawned, e.g. in test/suggestions.test.ts). Use plain quotes or escape with a backslash-backtick if a backtick must appear in the rendered output.

name: soft-archive-prune
description: ml prune defaults to soft-archive: stale records move to .mulch/archive/<domain>.jsonl with status: archived + archived_at fields. --hard opts into legacy true delete. Archive files start with a banner '# ARCHIVED — ...' that readExpertiseFile skips. ml restore <id> reverses (across domains, errors on ambiguity). ml search --archived opt-in includes archives in a separate '## <domain> (archived, N)' section with [ARCHIVED <date>] prefix per record.

content: readExpertiseFile skips lines whose trimmed form starts with '#' so archive files can prefix a banner without breaking JSONL parsing. Live JSONL never starts a record line with '#' (records are JSON objects), so the skip is a no-op for live files.

name: archive-record-shape
description: Soft-archived records carry status: 'archived' and archived_at: <iso-date> as optional BaseRecord fields. AJV schemas keep additionalProperties: false on live records, so live JSONL won't accidentally carry these — they only appear under .mulch/archive/. ml restore strips both fields before re-inserting to .mulch/expertise/.

name: supersession-demotion-prune
description: ml prune walks any record whose id is in another live record's supersedes array down the classification ladder (foundational→tactical→observational→archived), one tier per pass, stamping supersession_demoted_at. Staleness wins over supersession on overlap (no intermediate demotion stamp on a record that's about to be archived for staleness). --aggressive collapses straight to archived; --hard hard-deletes the bottomed-out record. Cross-domain by design.

content: Adding a field to a record requires updates in three schema sites: src/schemas/record.ts (BaseRecord interface), src/registry/builtins.ts (baseSchemaProps used by runtime AJV via type registry), and src/schemas/record-schema.ts (static reference schema, six oneOf entries — use Edit replace_all on the shared 'outcomes/dir_anchors' tail). Custom-type schemas in src/registry/custom.ts also need the field listed in the per-type properties block.

name: doctor-violation-record-counting
description: ml doctor domain-conformance must count records not messages. A single record violating both allowed_types AND required_fields pushes 2 messages to violations[]; the summary must use totalViolations (sum of perDomain.badCount, where badCount increments once per record via a recordViolated flag) — never violations.length. The detail block still lists every individual violation. See src/commands/doctor.ts checkDomainConformance.

name: summary-template-tokens
description: Custom-type summary templates accept either {field} or {{field}} syntax via TOKEN_RE alternation in src/registry/template.ts; tokens are validated at registry load against BASE_FIELDS + merged required/optional (including extends parent's fields), so unknown tokens fail loudly with the legal field list instead of rendering empty in ml prime output.

name: domain-rules-compatibility-check
description: Doctor check + helper findIncompatibleRequiredFields(config, domain, registry) in src/utils/domain-rules.ts surfaces domain.required_fields entries that no allowed type can hold (built-in/custom schemas are additionalProperties:false). Closed-schema BASE_RECORD_FIELDS set is exempted (id/type/classification/recorded_at/evidence/tags/relates_to/supersedes/outcomes/dir_anchors/supersession_demoted_at). Allowed types resolve to domain.allowed_types ?? registry.names().

name: ajv-additionalproperties-required-field-hint
description: findRejectedRequiredFields(validate.errors, requiredFields) in src/utils/domain-rules.ts scans Ajv ErrorObject[] for keyword==='additionalProperties' whose params.additionalProperty is in domain.required_fields. record.ts uses this to append a targeted hint on schema validation failure (both stdin and direct paths), instead of dumping oneOf/additionalProperties soup. Pre-fetches getRequiredFields(config, domain) BEFORE the validate() call so the rewritten error stays in one block.

name: anchor-validity-decay
description: ml prune --check-anchors demotes records whose file/dir/evidence anchors no longer resolve. computeAnchorValidity counts files[] + dir_anchors[] + evidence.file. Records with zero anchors are exempt (validFraction === null). Demotion stamps anchor_decay_demoted_at; can co-stamp with supersession_demoted_at on the same pass but only one tier. Staleness wins on overlap. Knobs: decay.anchor_validity.{threshold, grace_days, weight}.

content: Cascade tests for tier-demotion need recorded_at within all shelf-life windows: shelf_life.tactical=14d and shelf_life.observational=30d. Use daysAgo(8) — older than default grace_days (7) to trigger anchor-decay, but young enough that staleness doesn't short-circuit the ladder. recorded_at>14d would archive a tactical record on the second pass instead of demoting to observational.

name: recipe-discovery
description: src/utils/recipe-discovery.ts implements provider recipe discovery for ml setup. Resolution order: filesystem (.mulch/recipes/<name>.{ts,sh}) → npm (mulch-recipe-<name>) → built-in. Filesystem wins so orgs can override built-ins. ProviderRecipe shape: async install/check/remove(cwd) returning {success,message}. TS recipes use default export and are validated at load time via validateRecipeShape. Shell recipes invoked as <script> install|check|remove with cwd=project root and MULCH_RECIPE_NAME/MULCH_RECIPE_ACTION in env; stdout = success message; non-zero exit = failure with stderr surfaced. npm uses createRequire(import.meta.url) + require.resolve(pkg, { paths: [cwd] }) so resolution is scoped to project's node_modules. ml setup --list surfaces all discovered providers + shadow flags.

name: rank-command
description: ml rank [domain] returns confirmation-score-sorted records (highest first) as a flat cross-domain list. Filters: --type, --limit (default 10), --min-score (default 0). Differs from search --sort-by-score by not requiring a query and emitting a flat list rather than per-domain groups. JSON output keys: success/command/count/records[{domain,id,type,score,summary,record}].

name: supersession-cycle-detection
description: Iterative Tarjan SCC over the supersedes graph identifies records that participate in any cycle (A↔B, A→B→C→A); cycle members are excluded from supersededIds so they aren't demoted/archived together. Self-loops are filtered at edge collection so they don't form trivial SCCs. Iterative (no recursion) to survive long chains under noUncheckedIndexedAccess.

name: restore-dup-id-pre-check
description: ml restore performs a live-id pre-check before removeFromArchive (so a refusal doesn't strand the record), with a race-safe re-check inside restoreToExpertise's live-file lock. If the lock-side check fires, the archived record is re-archived as rollback so neither side is lost. The pre-check error message points users at `ml search --archived` to inspect the archived copy.

name: config-defaults-backfill
description: readConfig in src/utils/config.ts applies DEFAULT_CONFIG-style backfill for governance and classification_defaults so a hand-written minimal config (only declaring 'domains:') doesn't crash downstream consumers (doctor, status, prune, compact, prime) that destructure those required-by-type fields. Shallow-merge: partial user overrides keep explicit values, only missing keys get defaulted. yaml.load returning null/undefined for an empty file is also coerced to {} before defaulting.

name: cli-init-error-wrap
description: src/cli.ts wraps the top-level 'await initRegistryFromConfig()' in try/catch and prints a formatted 'Config error: <message>' line + a pointer to .mulch/mulch.config.yaml / 'mulch doctor', then process.exit(1). Without this, any throw from the registry init (bad extends/disabled_types) surfaces as a raw Bun stack trace because top-level await failures are unhandled. JSON mode (detected by argv scan, before Commander parses) emits outputJsonError('init', ...) instead.

name: hook-process-group-kill
description: src/utils/hooks.ts runOne uses node:child_process.spawn with detached:true (POSIX setsid → new process group, pgid==pid) instead of Bun.spawn. On timeout it calls process.kill(-pid, 'SIGKILL') so every descendant dies, not just the direct sh child. Reads stdout/stderr via 'data' events into accumulator strings; resolves on 'close' event. This avoids the prior hang where a forked child (e.g. backgrounded sleep) inherited the stdout fd and Promise.all([Response.text(), …]) never resolved after Bun's timeout fired only on sh.

content: Only pre-record and pre-prime are mutable hook events: their stdout JSON is parsed and replaces the payload for downstream scripts. pre-prune is a pre-* event but block-or-allow only — its stdout is ignored and a hook cannot reshape the candidate set. MUTABLE_EVENTS in src/utils/hooks.ts is the source of truth; runHooks JSDoc, src/schemas/config.ts comment, src/utils/config.ts INIT_CONFIG_OPTIONAL_KNOBS template, and README.md must agree.

name: recipe-default-export-strict
description: src/utils/recipe-discovery.ts loadFilesystemRecipe / loadNpmRecipe require mod.default to be present and to satisfy validateRecipeShape. Two distinct errors: 'has no default export' when mod.default is undefined, 'default export is not a valid ProviderRecipe' when present but missing async install/check/remove. The prior 'mod.default ?? mod' fallback accepted named-only exports against spec.

name: recipe-action-try-catch
description: src/commands/setup.ts wraps resolved.recipe.install/check/remove in try/catch around the await; on throw it constructs a RecipeResult with success:false and message 'recipe "<name>" <action> threw (<source>): <err>' so a thrown recipe surfaces as a normal failure (with --json {success:false, message}) instead of an unhandled top-level await error.

name: npm-shadow-probe
description: src/commands/setup.ts gatherProviderListings probes per-builtin npm shadow via createRequire(import.meta.url).resolve('mulch-recipe-<name>', {paths:[cwd]}) inside try/catch. Filesystem shadow still wins (resolveRecipe order: fs→npm→builtin), so shadowedBy reports 'filesystem-ts'/'filesystem-sh' if present, else 'npm' if installed, else undefined. Avoids enumerating node_modules.

name: dir-anchor-write-validation
description: src/utils/dir-anchors.ts splits validation: normalizeDirAnchor is read/write tolerant (strips trailing slash, leading './', backslash → '/', collapses repo-root markers to ''), never throws. assertWritableDirAnchor is the write-time gate — throws on absolute paths (/, C:\, D:/, \\server) and any '..' segment. ml record --dir-anchor calls assertWritableDirAnchor inside try/catch and surfaces formatted errors via outputJsonError or chalk.red without writing the record. fileLivesUnderDir keeps using only normalizeDirAnchor so legacy on-disk values match safely.

name: custom-type-name-rule-helper
description: src/registry/custom.ts exports CUSTOM_NAME_RE plus describeNameViolation() that returns a human-readable reason ('contains a hyphen', 'starts with a digit', 'must start with a lowercase letter', 'contains uppercase letters', 'contains a character outside [a-z0-9_]'). Used for type names AND for required/optional field names AND alias legacy names. Errors lead with the violation reason then 'Allowed: <NAMING_RULE>' instead of pasting the regex literal.

name: alias-foreign-base-field-check
description: src/registry/custom.ts builds BUILTIN_FIELDS = union of required+optional across BUILTIN_DEFS. Aliases legacy names that land in BUILTIN_FIELDS (and aren't in this type's mergedSeen — i.e. not inherited via extends) are rejected with 'collides with a built-in type's field'. Catches typos like aliases:{title:[name]} on a decision-extends type because 'name' is pattern's required field. Aliases legacy that's a base record field is still rejected separately by the BASE_FIELDS check.

name: strict-numeric-flag-parsing
description: CLI numeric flags (--limit, --min-score) must use a regex + Number() pair instead of parseInt/parseFloat. parseInt('10abc',10) returns 10 (silent strip); parseFloat('0.5xyz') returns 0.5; parseInt('3.7',10) truncates to 3. See parseStrictPositiveInt/parseStrictNonNegativeNumber in src/commands/rank.ts.

name: config-knob-range-validation
description: Range-check user-supplied config knobs (decay.anchor_validity.threshold/grace_days) at the call site that uses them — abort with formatted error before the operation runs — AND surface the same misconfiguration in 'ml doctor' so it's caught proactively. YAML accepts .nan/.inf, so include Number.isFinite checks alongside range bounds. See validateAnchorValidityConfig in src/schemas/config.ts wired into prune.ts and the new decay-config doctor check.

content: JSONL parse errors must include file:line context. readExpertiseFile wraps JSON.parse and throws 'Malformed JSONL at <file>:<line>: <reason>. Line: <preview>' instead of the engine's bare 'Unexpected token'.

name: onboard-version-decoupling
description: ml onboard renders package version (from getCurrentVersion()) in body text and a separate <!-- mulch-onboard:v<pkg> --> informational marker, while keeping a schema integer in <!-- mulch-onboard-schema:N --> as the sole driver of outdated-snippet detection. Patch-version bumps don't mark existing installs as outdated; only schema bumps do. SCHEMA_MARKER replaces the old VERSION_MARKER export (test imports updated). Schema bumped to 5 to migrate any legacy mulch-onboard-v:N installs.

title: Remove aider/gemini/windsurf default recipes
rationale: May 2026 docs audit found all three wrote to paths the provider runtime never reads. Aider auto-loads no markdown (needs .aider.conf.yml + read: directive). Gemini reads GEMINI.md at project root, not .gemini/settings.md. Windsurf uses .windsurf/rules/<name>.md directory with trigger: frontmatter, not flat .windsurf/rules.md. ml setup succeeded but produced no runtime effect. Hard-removed rather than deprecated since they were already broken; usage of these three providers in the wider ecosystem is also low compared to claude/cursor/codex.

name: toml-marker-comment-block
description: When ml setup codex needs to add a [[hooks.SessionStart]] entry to .codex/config.toml without adding a comment-preserving TOML library dependency, fence the managed region with TOML line comments: '# mulch:start' and '# mulch:end'. install() finds the markers and replaces the block (or appends if absent); check() looks for the markers; remove() strips the block plus the markers. Bun ships only Bun.TOML.parse (no stringify), and adding @iarna/toml or smol-toml just for one block is overkill. Same approach mirrors the HTML-comment marker pattern used for markdown files (markers.ts). Robust as long as users don't edit inside the fenced region.

name: owner-status-schema
description: Live AJV schemas reject status: 'archived' to preserve the archive/live invariant. The four schema sites (BaseRecord, builtins.baseSchemaProps, record-schema.ts oneOf branches, custom.ts properties block) only enumerate draft|active|deprecated in the status enum. Soft-archived records continue to bypass AJV — they live in .mulch/archive/<domain>.jsonl which readArchiveFile reads with allowUnknownTypes: true and which the validator never sees. BaseRecord.status TS type still includes 'archived' so archive.ts can stamp it.

name: spawn-injection-plain-format
description: ml prime --format plain is the spawn-injection contract for warren / other orchestrators that concatenate prime output into a coding agent's system prompt. Three rules: (1) no decorative title or ASCII underline (formatPrimeOutputPlain emits domain sections directly, not wrapped in a 'Project Expertise' header); (2) no trailing Session Close Protocol reminder (suppress getSessionEndReminder when format=plain in BOTH manifest and records branches of prime.ts); (3) preserve formatDomainExpertisePlain unchanged — it is snapshot-pinned via test/utils/format-regression.test.ts AND reused by ml search/query, so per-domain section structure (Conventions:/Patterns:/etc. with [mx-id] bulleted records) is the stable contract. Only formatPrimeOutputPlain (prime-only wrapper) is mutable.

Commander parses '--no-foo' flags as options.foo=false (defaults to true), NOT as options.noFoo=true. ml prime had options.noLimit !== true at line 200 of prime.ts, which was always true (undefined !== true), so --no-limit had been a silent no-op since the flag was added. Surfaced while implementing --dry-run because the dry-run JSON kept showing budgetTotal even with --no-limit.
resolution: Use options.limit !== false (the Commander-parsed attribute name) and update the PrimeOptions interface field to limit?: boolean, not noLimit?: boolean. Add a reminder comment at the field declaration site so the next person doesn't repeat the mistake. Verify locally with: const p = new Command(); p.option('--no-foo','x').action((o)=>console.log(o)); p.parse(['node','x','--no-foo']) → { foo: false }.

title: dry-run-skips-pre-prime-hooks
rationale: ml prime --dry-run is a UI/preview path (warren editor pane: 'would prime: N records, K tokens'). If pre-prime hooks ran on every preview, hook side effects (Slack posts via slack-post.sh, digest-then-confirm interactive prompts) would fire on every keystroke as the user edits a role's mulch: declaration. Per the soft-archive-prune precedent (mx-a7b234) where ml prune --dry-run also skips hooks, dry-run paths must skip hooks entirely. The dry-run branch in prime.ts is positioned AFTER record loading + --files/--context filtering but BEFORE runHooks('pre-prime'), so the JSON summary reflects the same kept-record set a real prime would emit while never invoking the hook chain. Trade-off: a hook that would normally drop records via stdout payload mutation (e.g. PII redaction) cannot influence dry-run output, but the warren UI is a preview, not the source-of-truth output that gets injected into the agent.

name: config-json-schema
description: src/schemas/config-schema.ts hand-authors a JSON Schema for MulchConfig mirroring src/schemas/config.ts. Conventions match record-schema.ts: every object literal carries type:'object' alongside required/properties for AJV strict; closed shapes use additionalProperties:false; open maps (domains, custom_types, aliases) use additionalProperties:<valueSchema>. Each property includes title, description, default (where applicable), enum (for unions), and minimum/maximum (for ranges). Exported via src/schemas/index.ts and src/index.ts. Powers warren's generic config UI via the upcoming `ml config schema` command (plan pl-6212).

name: config-subcommand-skeleton
description: ml config is the first multi-subcommand in mulch. Parent: program.command('config'); children chain via configCmd.command('schema'). Each subcommand registers its own .description()/.action(). Per the plan in pl-6212 (sd mulch-cbfb), additional subcommands (show/set/unset) attach to the same parent.

content: ml config schema prints the raw JSON Schema to stdout unconditionally — output is inherently a JSON Schema document, so the global --json flag is redundant rather than gating the format.

name: config-schema-golden-test
description: Golden test for ml config schema lives in test/commands/config.test.ts with the snapshot at test/commands/__snapshots__/config-schema.snapshot.json. Test runs from a fresh tmpdir (so initRegistryFromConfig falls back to built-ins-only, hermetic vs. dogfood .mulch/), spawns 'bun src/cli.ts config schema', and does byte-exact toBe comparison of stdout against the snapshot file. Regen with UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 bun test test/commands/config.test.ts. Mirrors the format-regression pattern in test/utils/format-regression.test.ts.

content: Biome formatter excludes test/**/__snapshots__/** in biome.json files.includes — snapshot files use JSON.stringify(x, null, 2) 2-space indent which conflicts with biome's tab indentStyle. Without the exclusion, lint would fight any committed JSON snapshot.

name: config-show-schema-default-fallback
description:
ml config show --path

walks the dot-notation path through the read config; if the value is undefined, falls back to walkSchemaDefault on configSchema. Leaves return their declared 'default'; non-leaf nodes recurse into 'properties' and collect child defaults so '--path prime' (after unset) yields {default_mode: 'full'}. Open maps (additionalProperties as object) are followed when 'properties' has no matching key, supporting paths like domains..allowed_types. Errors with 'Path

not found in config and has no schema default' when neither config nor schema produces a value.

content: ml config show emits JSON unconditionally (no --json flag of its own; the global --json flag is accepted as a no-op). Output is the readConfig() result for the full dump, or the resolved value at --path <dot.path>. Symmetric with ml config schema's always-JSON contract — both subcommands target warren UI consumption.

name: config-set-atomic-write
description: ml config set: validates dot-notation path against the schema (rejects closed-shape misses with known-keys hint), YAML-parses <value>, mutates the config under withFileLock, validates the full result against configSchema with ajv (strict:false to accept hand-authored draft-07 schema), then writes via temp-file-and-rename. AJV errors are surfaced with the schema field's title appended (e.g. 'must be integer (Soft target)'). Open-map paths (domains.<name>.<field>, custom_types.<name>.<field>) auto-create intermediate objects via setAtPath; closed-shape unknowns error before write.

content: Ajv import in src/commands/config.ts uses 'import Ajv, { type ErrorObject } from "ajv"' — Bun handles the ESM/CJS interop without the default-shim that NodeNext+ts-node needs. AJV is constructed with 'strict: false' so the hand-authored draft-07 configSchema (which has no formats and uses additionalProperties:false on closed shapes) compiles without strict-mode warnings. Matches the existing pattern in src/registry/type-registry.ts.

name: config-unset-schema-aware-prune
description: ml config unset: deletes the leaf at the dot-notation path, then walks the path back up pruning any empty ancestor object whose key is NOT in its parent schema node's `required` list (isRequiredInSchema()). So `unset search.boost_factor` leaves no orphan `search: {}` in the YAML (search is optional at root, so the empty parent is pruned), while `unset domains.warren` leaves `domains: {}` intact (domains is in root.required). Required-field removals — e.g. `unset governance.max_entries` — are rejected by AJV validation after readConfig re-applies defaults to governance (so governance is non-empty post-delete and pruning stops there, but max_entries is still required). Idempotent: missing parent or already-unset leaf returns early without rewriting the file. Same closed-shape gate (validatePathInSchema) as `set`.

content: AJV `required` validation errors report the PARENT's instancePath, not the missing child's. So formatAjvError() in src/commands/config.ts appends the parent section's schema title (e.g. 'Governance limits') rather than the missing leaf's title ('Soft target'). Tests asserting schema-titled error messages on required-property failures must check for the section title, not the leaf field title.

name: config-worktree-concurrency-tests
description: test/commands/config.test.ts adds two integration blocks for ml config: worktree (real git worktree add fixture; verifies schema/show read main config, set/unset write main config but leave worktree checkout copy untouched, .tmp/.lock cleanup, lock spans worktree boundary) and concurrency (disjoint-keys → no lost writes via withFileLock-serialized read-modify-write; same-key contention → last-writer-wins, final ∈ inputs, never garbled YAML; set + unset interleave on disjoint keys; invalid + valid concurrent writes — invalid rejected without corrupting on-disk config; .tmp/.lock cleanup; final YAML round-trips through ml config show). Uses runCliAsync (Bun.spawn + new Response().text()) for true concurrency vs spawnSync. realpath() the tmp dir on macOS so resolveWorktreeRoot's resolved path matches.

name: audit-command-surface
description: ml audit splits into src/utils/audit.ts (compute/types) and src/commands/audit.ts (CLI/format). Verdict bands: PASS/WARN/FAIL per metric. --ci forces JSON output and sets exit 1 on any FAIL; --suggest groups records into archive/revise/attribute buckets with copy-paste ml prune/ml edit commands; --domain scopes; --ignore-domains merges with audit.ignore_domains. audit.thresholds (global) + audit.per_domain.<name> (overrides) are resolved via resolveAuditThresholds() in src/schemas/config.ts.

content: Floater rate verdict uses an asymmetric WARN/FAIL band: PASS when <= floater_max, WARN when <= 2x floater_max, FAIL above. V1_PLAN expects floaters to drop after attribution gates land in v0.11, so the FAIL band should fire only for flagrantly unattributed corpora — symmetric bands would FAIL every project pre-v0.11.

name: status-age-and-rotting
description: ml status surfaces per-domain oldest→newest recorded range and a ROTTING flag when newest record age exceeds classification_defaults.shelf_life.observational. formatStatusOutput accepts optional oldestRecorded/newestRecorded/rotting/rottingDays per domain (back-compat: omitted = legacy line). status.ts computes rotting via (now - newestRecorded) > observational shelf life; empty domains never rot. JSON output adds oldest_recorded/newest_recorded/rotting/rotting_days + top-level shelf_life.

name: active-work-resolver-chain
description: src/utils/active-work.ts ships symmetric per-tracker resolvers (seedsResolver, ghResolver, linearResolver, beadResolver) returning ResolverHit{tracker, matches[], source}. resolveActiveWork() runs all resolvers, applies one-match-only auto-link, emits stderr-formatted warnings on multi-match, and lets explicit overrides short-circuit any resolver. seeds/bead resolvers read .seeds/issues.jsonl and .beads/issues.jsonl for status='in_progress' (preferred signal) before falling back to branch-name regex. linear/gh are branch-only by default; gh tries 'gh pr view --json number' first when available. Empty-string override is treated as not-set so a forwarded undefined CLI flag doesn't suppress the resolver.

content: Tracker ID regex conventions in src/utils/active-work.ts: seeds = /[a-z][a-z0-9]*-[a-f0-9]{4,8}/ (lowercase prefix + 4-8 hex), linear = /[A-Z]{2,}[A-Z0-9]*-\d+/ (requires 2+ uppercase chars so single-letter prefixes don't collide with seeds-style IDs), bead = /(?:bd|bead)-\d+/i (case-insensitive, normalized to lowercase on output), gh = /(?:gh-|#)\d+/i (requires explicit gh-/# prefix to avoid matching arbitrary numbers embedded in branch slugs).

name: session-close-helper-centralized
description: Single getSessionEndReminder(format) in src/utils/format.ts now feeds prime footer, onboard snippet, Cursor recipe, and Codex recipe. PrimeFormat extended to SessionCloseStyle = PrimeFormat | 'embedded'. The 'embedded' variant produces a markdown ### Before You Finish block (h3) for embedding in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.cursor/rules. Adding a new close-session surface is now a one-line call.

content: Close-session prose phrasing is conditional ('If you discovered ...') not ritualistic ('you MUST run this checklist … NEVER skip this'). The 🚨 marker is preserved in markdown and plain prime-footer variants (memory anchor for edit-saturated contexts per V1_PLAN §5.2) and intentionally omitted from the 'embedded' snippet variant (which lives inside a session-start CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md section, not at the end of a long context window).

name: prime-auto-context-scope
description: ml prime in full mode auto-context-scopes to the agent's working set when not jsonMode/isScoped/--all and inside a git repo. Signals: getActiveFiles (git status --porcelain -uall) + resolveActiveWork (seeds/gh/linear/bead). filterByActiveContext keeps records when (a) no file/dir/evidence-tracker anchors, (b) files/dir_anchors match changed, or (c) evidence.{seeds,gh,linear,bead} equals an active tracker id. Records load unfiltered first so auto-flip-to-manifest threshold sees the full corpus. Stderr prints 'prime: scoped to N of M ...; --all for full corpus'.

content: git status --porcelain -uall is the right invocation for per-untracked-file listing — the default collapses untracked directories to a single '??' entry, which breaks file-anchor matching downstream. Used in getActiveFiles (src/utils/git.ts) for prime auto-context-scope.

Auto-magic behaviors in ml prime (auto-flip to manifest, auto-context-scope) are skipped under --json mode so machine consumers see deterministic output regardless of the caller's git state. Detection / scoping should be a UX layer, not a contract.

name: prime-trust-tier-ranking
description: ml prime sorts surfaced records by trust score = (★ count * star_weight) + classification_weight before applyBudget so highest-trust records survive truncation. Defaults: star=100, foundational=50, tactical=20, observational=10. prime.tier_weights config overrides any subset; partial overrides inherit the rest from DEFAULT_PRIME_TIER_WEIGHTS via resolveTierWeights() spread merge. Within-tier ties preserve insertion order via decorate-sort-undecorate so run-to-run output stays stable.

name: prime-why-surfaced-suffix
description: ml prime appends a 'why: ...' suffix to each surfaced record picked in priority order: file_match (files/dir_anchors overlap with active changed-file set), tracker_match (evidence.{seeds,gh,linear,bead} matches active-work resolver), stars (★N confirmations from outcomes), recent (recorded < 7d ago), universal (no anchors, no other signal). Implemented via buildSurfaceAnnotations() → Map<recordId, suffix>; formatters apply via post-processing (compact: append per line; markdown/plain: regex match leading [mx-N] tag; xml: why='...' attribute on record element). JSON/manifest skip annotations.

content: Optional config sections that add NEW defaults (e.g. prime.tier_weights) must avoid breaking ml config show --path on existing tests that synthesize an unset section's leaf defaults — adding a sibling field under prime expands the synthesized object, so the matching expect() block needs updating in lockstep. Drop overly-strict 'required: [...]' arrays at the same time when adding optional siblings or downstream config files that only set the parent key will start failing schema validation.

Annotation maps (Map<recordId, string>) thread cleanly into formatDomainExpertise* via optional trailing parameters — keeps search/query callers untouched while letting prime inject per-record suffixes. For markdown/plain formats, post-process the def.formatMarkdown output by regex-matching the leading [mx-N] tag rather than threading through TypeDefinition.formatMarkdown signature (which would ripple into Phase 2 custom types). Trade-off: bullets without ids skip annotation, but pre-id corpora are vanishingly rare.

name: archive-reason-field
description: Every code path that writes to .mulch/archive/<domain>.jsonl now stamps an archive_reason ('stale' | 'superseded' | 'anchor_decay' | 'manual' | 'compacted'; free-text suffix permitted). archiveRecords(domain, records, now, reason, cwd?) takes reason as a required positional arg; records that arrive with a pre-stamped archive_reason keep theirs (so prune's bottom-out path can per-record-stamp 'superseded'/'anchor_decay' while the same call handles 'stale' records via the param default). stripArchiveFields() strips archive_reason alongside status+archived_at. ml restore captures the original reason BEFORE stripping so rollback-on-race preserves the original and the success message can echo it. Field is optional on BaseRecord — back-compat with archives written pre-mulch-b41a. Live record schemas (additionalProperties: false) don't list archive_reason; v1.0-frozen envelope stays intact.

name: archive-command
description: ml archive <domain> [id] (or --records <a,b>) soft-archives specific live records without waiting for ml prune. Required --reason <text> is stamped on disk as 'manual: <text>' via the archive_reason field; --dry-run previews; --json emits { success, command: 'archive', domain, dryRun, archived: [{id, type, summary, reason}], kept }. ID resolution reuses resolveRecordId (prefix/mx-/bare-hash) like ml delete and ml restore. Live-file read+rewrite happens inside a single withFileLock(getExpertisePath(domain)) acquisition; the archiveRecords() write acquires its own archive-file lock after the live lock drops (matches ml prune phase 3). Symmetric to ml restore.

name: prune-dry-run-granular-default
description: ml prune --dry-run prints per-record IDs + reasons by default; --explain adds anchor breakdown on top; --quiet keeps only the Total line; --json output unchanged. Reason vocabulary matches archive_reason (stale|superseded|anchor_decay).

name: pre-compact-hook
description: pre-compact lifecycle hook (HOOK_EVENTS in src/schemas/config.ts; MUTABLE_EVENTS + BLOCKING_EVENTS in src/utils/hooks.ts) lets a script print { replacement: <RecordShape> } on stdout to override the mechanical mergeRecords() output during ml compact. Payload is { domain, type, records[] }. When absent, falls back to mechanical merge. The replacement is auto-stamped with supersedes:[old_ids] when the hook omits it, then validated via getRegistry().validator and IDed via generateRecordId before write.

name: compact-soft-archive
description: ml compact (handleAuto + handleApply in src/commands/compact.ts) soft-archives source records via archiveRecords(domain, sources, now, 'compacted') AFTER the live file lock releases — archive takes its own lock on .mulch/archive/<domain>.jsonl. ml restore <id> round-trips a compacted source back to live. Mirrors prune's stale-archive pattern.

title: Release v0.10.0 — visibility + prime overhaul
rationale: Twelve PR-sized steps deliver V1_PLAN section 3 visibility plus prime overhaul: ml audit + age-aware status (measurement surface), active-work resolver chain + centralized close-session prose (foundational primitives), three-slice ml prime overhaul (lead with contract, manifest auto-flip >100/>5, context-scope default, trust-tier ranking + why-surfaced suffix), curation-surface gaps (ml archive, archive_reason, granular prune --dry-run, ml compact overhaul with pre-compact hook + soft-archive originals). One behavioral breaking change: ml prime default auto-flips to manifest above 100 records or 5 domains; downstream consumers pin --full --all for legacy shape. Live BaseRecord schema unchanged; archive_reason lives only on archived rows.

description: ml record <domain> --type release_decision --version <v> short-circuits to the program-level --version handler in src/cli.ts (early-exit branch that prints VERSION and process.exit()) instead of recording the custom-type field. The custom-type field flag is registered on the subcommand (src/commands/record.ts:510-518) but Commander resolves the program-level -v/--version first, so the field never receives the value and no record is written. Surfaced while recording the v0.10.0 release_decision: ml output was a bare '0.10.0' line and the JSONL was unchanged.
resolution: Workaround: use --batch <file> to bypass the flag-parsing conflict — JSON payload carries the version field directly. Long-term fix: detect `record` as the active subcommand in the rawArgs scan before consuming --version, or skip the global -v/--version short-circuit when a custom type declares a 'version' field.

name: tracker-agnostic-audit
description: ml audit treats seeds/gh/linear/bead symmetrically: evidence.with_tracker is a Record<TrackerName, number>, tracker_citations replaces seed_citations, and TRACKERS from active-work.ts is the single source of truth for iteration. Human output omits per-tracker lines that are 0 so seeds-only repos still print one tracker line. Citation display uses formatTrackerId(): seeds bare (back-compat), gh -> 'gh#42', linear/bead -> '<tracker>:<id>'. gh/linear/bead lack local indexes today, so their citations bucket into status_counts.unresolved (not 'missing' — missing is reserved for seeds ids absent from .seeds/issues.jsonl). Back-compat: evidence.with_seeds and seed_citations stay populated for one release, filtered to seeds-only entries so old consumers don't see foreign tracker data.

name: pre-init-config-fallback
description: Recipes in src/commands/setup.ts and the snippet builder in src/commands/onboard.ts read prime.session_close from mulch.config.yaml at install/check time, but setup/onboard may run before `ml init` — wrap readConfig() in try/catch and fall back to undefined so the default preset is applied rather than failing the command. Module-level constants that depended on config (CURSOR_RULE_CONTENT, CODEX_SECTION) become async builder functions; the cursor recipe's exact-match check also calls the builder so config-driven content doesn't false-fail equality.

content: When getSessionEndReminder (or any prime-footer helper) can return an empty string — e.g. style: none — the call site MUST guard `if (reminder.length > 0) output += \`\n\n${reminder}\`` before concatenation. Leading-blank-line concatenation otherwise leaves trailing whitespace in the rendered prime output when the footer is suppressed.

name: pi-config-namespace
description: pi.* config namespace in MulchConfig + JSON schema is consumed by the in-tree @os-eco/pi-mulch extension (extensions/pi/index.ts). Knobs: auto_prime, scope_load.{enabled,budget,debounce_ms}, tools, commands, agent_end_widget. extensions/pi/lib/config.ts exports resolvePiConfig(user) which merges over DEFAULT_PI_CONFIG, and readPiConfig(cwd) which calls readConfig() — read on every hook invocation so edits take effect without restart. The pi block is fully ajv-validated via configSchema, settable via 'ml config set pi.<path> <value>'.

content: content=Add pi as optional peerDependency (^0.74.0) + typebox (^1.1.0) in package.json with peerDependenciesMeta marking both optional so CLI-only users see no peer-dep noise. Mirror in devDependencies so 'bun install' resolves them for typecheck/lint. The pi manifest { extensions: ['./extensions/pi/index.ts'] } points pi at the extension.
